summary: App with login authentication, role-based access control, sidebar navigation, and user management. Admins have full access including a Settings menu to manage users (create/edit/delete with name, password, role). Regular users have limited access with no Settings visibility.
architectural_notes: Authentication is internal credential check (username + password), not a third-party identity service
Users stored in app backend with name, password, and role fields

existing_features.title: Replace the Internet Identity authentication gate in the frontend with a username/password login screen. The login screen should show the app logo/name, a username field, a password field, and a Sign In button. On successful authentication, store the logged-in user (id, username, role) in React context/state accessible app-wide.

Add a Settings page accessible only to admin users. Add a 'Settings' navigation link in the Sidebar that is only rendered when the logged-in user has the Admin role. Regular users should not see or be able to navigate to the Settings page.

Build the Settings page with a User Management section. Display a table of all users showing their username and role. Provide buttons to: (1) Create a new user (opens a dialog with username, password, and role fields), (2) Edit an existing user (opens a pre-filled dialog for username, password, and role), (3) Delete a user (with a confirmation prompt). Prevent deletion of the currently logged-in admin user.

Enforce role-based access control across the app. Regular users (role: User) should not see the Settings sidebar link, cannot access admin-only pages, and all admin-only action buttons (add/edit/delete) on AssetsPage, PartsInventoryPage, RepairTicketsPage, etc. remain gated. Base access checks on the locally stored logged-in user role instead of the previous Internet Identity-based role check.

Update the Header component to display the logged-in user's username and role, and replace the Internet Identity logout button with a logout action that clears the local session and returns the user to the login screen.

new_feature_requests.summary: Login screen with username and password authentication
Settings menu in sidebar accessible only to admins
User management inside Settings: create, view, edit, delete users with name, password, and role (admin or regular user)
Role-based access control: admins get full app access, regular users have limited access with no Settings visibility

Add an AppUser data model to the backend with fields: id (Nat), username (Text), passwordHash (Text), and role (variant { Admin; User }). Store users in a stable HashMap. Seed one default admin account (username: 'admin', password: 'admin123') on first deploy if no users exist.

Expose backend functions for user management: login(username, password) returning a session token or error; getUsers() returning all users (admin only); createUser(username, password, role) (admin only); updateUser(id, username, password, role) (admin only); deleteUser(id) (admin only).
